dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3768 IS: Advancing Better Options for Dwellings Everywhere Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Durbin (for himself and Ms. Duckworth) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs

form.official-title: To direct the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to conduct a grant competition for the development of single- and multi-family homes, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Advancing Better Options for Dwellings Everywhere Act or the ABODE Act.

subsection.text: The Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (in this section referred to as the Secretary), in consultation with the Department of Energy and the Environmental Protection Agency, shall—

paragraph.text: conduct a grant competition to provide grants to academic organizations, nonprofit organizations, and for-profit or nonprofit mission-driven developers to develop, or rehabilitate existing, single- and multi-family homes—

subparagraph.text: for households with an income that is not more than 50 percent of the area median income; and

clause.text: increasing resiliency, energy efficiency, accessibility for individuals with disabilities; and
selecting projects that are built to scale; and

paragraph.text: study the short- and long-term savings of the resiliency and energy efficiency measures that were implemented in the homes described in paragraph (1).

subsection.text: A grant awarded under this section shall be awarded at completion of the contract for building a pre-determined number of homes that abide by pre-determined resiliency and energy efficiency measures.
The Secretary shall prioritize grant awards that—

paragraph.text: develop or rehabilitate homes in areas with a severe affordable housing shortage;
focus on quality, durability and maintenance costs, and neighborhood design compatibility; or
develop or rehabilitate homes with universal design that provides access for individuals with disabilities.

subsection.text: Not later than 2 years after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the competition and study conducted under this section, which shall include—

paragraph.text: a description of the projects funded and completed;
the number and sales or rental price, as applicable, of affordable houses built; and
the results of the study described in subsection (a)(2).

subsection.text: There is authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out this section.
